mechanism_narrative: Higher retail sales levels capture robust consumer expenditure that translates directly into corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns following elevated RSAFS are not positive in more than 50% of months, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 4% indicates a tight labor market that generates wage income growth and sustains aggregate consumer demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of instances, or the mean excess return over cash is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated nominal interest rates and high inflation jointly compresses corporate real margins and equity valuations; when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2% alongside CPIAUCSL above 250, S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are negative.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS > 2% and CPIAUCSL > 250, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative less than 55% of the time, or the interaction term is insignificant (p > 0.05) in a pooled regression.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, increases in the federal funds rate raise discount rates applied to equity cash flows and increase debt service costs, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: During tightening cycles, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of weeks following rate increases, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production above 108 reflects strong manufacturing momentum and capacity utilization that supports earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO exceeds 108, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of instances, or the Rank IC drops below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1,150 thousand units signal robust residential investment and construction spillovers into broad demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1150, forward 60-day returns are not significantly positive versus the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05), or positive return frequency falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high payroll employment above 150,000 thousand and low unemployment below 4% create a wage-demand loop that boosts corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS > 150000 and UNRATE < 4%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive less than 55% of the time, or the joint signal underperforms the standalone PAYEMS signal.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index above 255 signals elevated inflation that erodes real earnings and tightens monetary policy expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL exceeds 255, 5-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of instances, or the mean negative return is insignificant (t-test p > 0.10).
